evals.prompt_summary: Reverse proxy ForwardedHeaders for HTTPS discovery
without_skill_failures: Output mentions that the ForwardedHeaders middleware must run before IdentityServer middleware in the pipeline
Output includes adding proxy IP addresses to KnownProxies or KnownNetworks for production, or explains the default localhost-only trust
Output mentions that the signing key store must also be shared (e.g., EF operational store instead of default file-system) for multi-instance deployments

evals.prompt_summary: Health checks for discovery and JWKS endpoints
without_skill_failures: Output includes a health check class implementing IHealthCheck that tests the discovery endpoint by finding and invoking the IdentityServerConstants.EndpointNames.Discovery handler
Output includes a health check class implementing IHealthCheck that tests the JWKS endpoint by finding and invoking the IdentityServerConstants.EndpointNames.Jwks handler
Output injects IEnumerable<Hosting.Endpoint> and IHttpContextAccessor into the health check classes

evals.prompt_summary: OpenTelemetry with Prometheus and production tracing
without_skill_failures: Output adds the 'Duende.IdentityServer' meter (or Telemetry.ServiceName) to the metrics configuration
Output adds only IdentityServerConstants.Tracing.Basic as the tracing source for production, and does NOT include Stores, Validation, Cache, or Services sources
Output changes the logging level for Duende.IdentityServer to Warning (or higher) in the appsettings.json or production appsettings
Output explains that Trace and Debug levels expose sensitive data like tokens and must not be used in production
Output configures options.Logging.UnhandledExceptionLoggingFilter to return false for OperationCanceledException when ctx.RequestAborted.IsCancellationRequested is true

evals.prompt_summary: Distributed caching for multi-instance OIDC flows
without_skill_failures: Output explains that the OIDC state data formatter stores external provider state in IDistributedCache, and without a shared cache, state created on instance A cannot be validated on instance B
Output mentions at least two of the four features that require distributed cache: OIDC state data, JWT replay cache, device flow throttling, or PAR authorization parameter store
Output sets ForwardLimit to 2 (or the correct number matching the proxy chain depth), explaining that the default of 1 only processes headers from a single proxy
Output adds both proxy IP addresses to KnownProxies or their network ranges to KnownNetworks
Output includes ForwardedHeaders.XForwardedHost in addition to XForwardedProto to fix the incorrect host name
Output explains that each proxy in the chain must be trusted (added to KnownProxies/KnownNetworks) for the forwarded headers to be processed
